{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"GoHighLevel tries to bundle a lot of features together, but any integrations with third-party tools are surface-level and clunky. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Once Monday is configured, and the team is trained, it can help you stay on top of your work. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Because Monday is very customizable, it requires proper team onboarding from the person setting up the account. If an implementation is not done right and there's no onboarding, you can expect that almost certainly the team will not adopt it. So if you go down the path of using Monday, you need to make sure that you invest in proper team training and hand-holding.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Monday has a very steep learning curve and is a tool you'd likely need a consultant to help you set up. Some teams have even reported spending over $20,000 on implementation, only to end up with a system that didn’t work as they imagined. This steep learning curve is especially important to keep in mind if you're a small team. Because of that, we typically recommend Monday more for enterprises or teams that have someone more technical who’s familiar with processes and software implementation. It's more for highly complex projects that require very deep customization. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Monday is not truly innovative. They followed Asana and built a competitor, but there was nothing revolutionary about Monday. They always excelled in their marketing with their bright colors, but in terms of the actual user experience, many people have felt like Monday has fallen short. Users report having  consistent performance issues. Monday has also gone down the path of trying to be an all-in-one tool, similar to ClickUp, But they have taken it to another level. They have so many features at this point that it feels like they don't even know themselves what they are and who they're for. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Monday's AI feels confusing, and ultimately not very useful. The AI features that exist feel like light automation layers tied to columns rather than anything that meaningfully speeds up real work. Combined with clunky workflows, a high learning curve, and a $2,400/year price tag if you go over free credits, the ROI simply isn’t there. A lot of users also complained that they will build out these robust workflows, and then if they go over the usage, you can only upgrade for the entire year rather than paying for the overage for that month. So you're basically faced with either not being able to rely on the AI for the rest of the month or paying $2,400 for the whole year. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion was one of the first tools that let you build your own systems, it’s very customizable. But that innovation doesn’t make it a good project management tool. Notion markets itself as a great project management tool, but it doesn’t highlight the steep learning curve. So while it’s flexible, it’s not actually helpful for the average user. It’s best suited for a small subset of people who want to build something fully custom. AKA the average business owner should not be using Notion for projects as it will serve as a major distraction.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion launched AI Agents and gave them these cute characters and these cute hats, but the overall response with the functionality has been mixed. Overall a lot of people have been disappointed with the functionality saying it's not as good or comprehensive as they thought it would be and it sometimes falls short. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"While some people like to argue that Notion is easy to learn, they are people that are typically more technical and passionate about learning about databases.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"If you don't have interest in learning how to build apps and you have an interest in just managing your projects, you are definitely better off using another project management tool. Notion has a very steep learning curve, and people are often surprised when it doesn't work as easily as everybody makes it seem.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Team adoption with Notion for project management tends to be extremely low. The person who built the system usually had to invent their own structure, and often that structure only makes sense to them. When they try to onboard the rest of the team, it creates a ton of friction.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"On top of that, most people don’t think about scalability aka how this system will hold up as the team grows to 5–10+ people. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion is the most unproductive project management tool. The reason is because you have to build it yourself from scratch, which means you need to learn how databases and relational databases work. Our argument is that if you are running a small business, there is no world where you should need to learn about these things, and instead focus on running your business. You should use a tool that out of the box does project management out of the box. Notion is more of a distraction.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Brex's interface is fine, but the banking UX just doesn't feel like the main product.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"It's relatively modern, and you can usually get to what you need without fighting with it, but once you're actually trying to do banking things, it feels like you're navigating a platform that was built for spend/expense management first, with banking being added in as an afterthought.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"The biggest friction is that your cash experience is split across checking, treasury, and vault, so even simple questions like \"where's our money?\" can require some digging. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Akiflow gives you a central inbox for all your tasks from different apps and lets you time block by dragging tasks onto your calendar, but the planning process is manual and can quickly get overwhelming. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Akiflow clears up a lot of mental clutter if your main problem is having tasks scattered across different apps. You get everything in one inbox, so you always know what needs attention and don’t have to hunt through email, Slack, and project management tools. The command bar and keyboard shortcuts make it easy to triage and time block your day, so you can quickly sort and act on what matters.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"But once your tasks are consolidated, you still have to manually plan and organize them every day. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Akiflow nails integrations for daily planning. You can pull in tasks from a wide range of apps like Trello, Slack, Notion, Todoist, Google Calendar, Asana, ClickUp, Gmail, and Outlook, so everything lands in one central inbox without you needing to jump between tabs. Even if your tools aren’t natively supported, Akiflow’s Zapier connector lets you set up simple custom workflows with any app that works with Zapier, so you’re not stuck waiting for official integrations.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"You don’t have to babysit the process, tasks from all connected tools show up in Akiflow automatically, and you can manage them right from the app. This is especially strong if you’re juggling a mess of different platforms and just want everything in one place. The only real gap is that the Zapier integration is basic (create-task only), and completed tasks in Akiflow don’t sync back to the original app, so you might have to check things off twice. But for getting your tasks, calendars, and communication streams centralized fast and reliably, nothing else in this category handles integrations better.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Akiflow does not support automatic or real-time adaptive rescheduling. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Akiflow does not support AI auto-scheduling. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Morgen Calendar only supports manual task scheduling, so you still have to drag and drop tasks onto your calendar yourself, and if your schedule changes or you miss a deadline, you need to rearrange everything by hand. There's mention that a Dynamic Time Blocking feature might be coming, but right now, you get no automatic day-building or AI-driven time-blocking based on your priorities or availability.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"If you're just looking to dabble with daily planning and don't have a ton of tasks, this setup works, but you'll feel the friction quickly if you want your day to rebuild itself or keep up with shifting priorities. You still have to babysit your schedule to keep it accurate, and any workflow that depends on automatic rescheduling or AI-driven task placement falls apart. This is fine for personal use with light task loads, but you'll outgrow it fast if you expect your planner to build your day for you.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Routine makes basic planning confusing and frustrating right from the start. Instead of feeling intuitive, you end up guessing how to do simple things and running into trial and error, even for core actions like scheduling or organizing tasks.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"You can accidentally access database-level settings and even delete core structures, which should never happen in a daily planner. The labels system tries to add organization but just piles on more complexity without making anything clearer. The separation between tasks and notes is messy, so you're never really sure where things go.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"The interface might look good, but the actual experience is clunky and unfriendly. You still have to manually rearrange your whole schedule if you add a longer or urgent task, so nothing about the planning flow feels easy or quick to pick up. This does not support a clear or intuitive planning methodology.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Routine makes it harder to keep your head clear. The interface looks good, but the actual experience is messy and confusing. You get features like labels and database-style structures that should help you organize, but they just add mental load without giving you real insight. It's too easy to get lost or even accidentally delete core parts of your setup.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"You still have to manually rearrange your entire schedule when priorities change, because nothing auto-adjusts. The way tasks and notes blend together just adds to the confusion, and you never feel like your priorities or next steps are actually clear. Instead of simplifying your day, Routine leaves you with more clutter and frustration.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Routine only connects with a limited set of tools right now, mainly Google Workspace and Notion. You can add tasks from your email or Notion, but if you rely on other apps, you're out of luck. There's no real support for project management tool integrations yet, and bigger platforms like Slack, Asana, and ClickUp are still just \"coming soon\", not actually available.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"There's also no API, so you can't build custom workflows or connect to anything outside of what's already included. If you need to centralize tasks, calendars, and communication from multiple sources, you still have to do most of it manually. This makes Routine feel pretty basic for integrations, especially compared to what you'd expect from a daily planner aimed at professionals.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Routine does not support adaptive rescheduling. When your plans change or you need to add an urgent task, you still have to manually rearrange your entire schedule to fit the new task in. Nothing shifts automatically, so you end up fighting with the tool instead of trusting it to keep your day on track. This breaks down fast if your day isn't static.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Routine does not support AI auto-scheduling. You have to manually drag and drop every task onto your calendar and rearrange your entire day yourself if anything changes. Even when you try to add an urgent or longer task, nothing shifts automatically, every adjustment is on you, and the app does not move or reprioritize tasks based on availability or priority. This makes time-blocking tedious and unreliable, especially if you're expecting your planner to build your day for you.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"If you’re managing simple tasks, Trello can help you stay on top of daily productivity. But once you need visibility into long-term planning or even anything that spans more than a week, you'll likely hit its limits fast. You'll end up bouncing between Trello and Slack for alignment, and you still won’t get any meaningful reporting or analytics to understand the bigger picture of your business. In that sense, it can actually make you less productive.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Trello is incredibly easy to learn because it is so basic. That said, you can't do full-fledged project management using Trello. It's more task management for very small teams, like under three people than a full fledged project management tool. Trello lacks task dependencies, meaning it does not track relationships between tasks (such as one task needing to finish before another can start) - a core feature of project management.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Trello’s AI is pretty basic compared to more advanced project management tools. They’ve added Atlassian Intelligence for things like summarizing, drafting, and organizing tasks, plus some expanded Butler automation  but it all stays in the lane of simple workflow automation and light writing help. There’s no real analytics, predictive insights, or deeper decision-making here.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Trello is one of the easiest tools for teams to adopt because it is so incredibly basic and simple to use. Team members just need to understand how to use a basic Kanban board: open the board, find their task, move it through the workflow, or leave a quick comment. If they can do that, they’ve basically already adopted Trello.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Trello was once innovative but they are now stagnant. They invented the Kanban view for project management but this is just now table stakes in any tool out there.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Firefox sets itself apart with a privacy-focused approach, but you immediately run into friction from its limited extension support and lower compatibility with websites. You lose access to the huge ecosystem of Chrome extensions, unless developers specifically rebuild them for Firefox, which rarely happens. That means you're stuck with fewer options, and there's a real chance that some sites simply won't work right.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"If you pick Firefox as your main browser, you'll probably find yourself reaching for a second browser just to get full features or to use sites that don't play nice. The interface doesn't get in your way, but the constant need to switch browsers or deal with missing tools breaks any sense of a truly efficient or seamless workflow. You can get by, but it never feels smooth or fully reliable for heavy or demanding use.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Prior to AI, Asana was a headache to keep up-to-date because every field needed to be manually updated. But now with their AI Chat feature, it has become easier to push back deadlines and help you stay on top of your work. That said, it still requires you to keep an eye on things which ends up feeling like you're babysitting the project management tool. With their AI teammates, they can assist you in getting work done so it's trending in the right direction.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Asana is not set up out of the box, so your team will not be able to adopt it until its configured. You will need dedicated documentation and training sessions with your team to make sure they get properly onboarded. Team adoption tends to be slightly lower for Asana because it can feel overwhelming if you don't know the exact system your company uses. Many teams that implement it don’t actually stick with it unless there’s a lot of handholding and consistent enforcement from management to ensure it gets used. Not to mention a lot of implementations are not done properly, which leads to a lot of confusion among teams and resistance when it comes to adoption. This is why it's incredibly important that if you use Asana, you work with a consultant or somebody who has set it up before to make sure that it is as useful to the team as possible. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Asana has a good user experience, but it’s so customizable that you’ll likely need a dedicated person or agency to set it up properly. If you want to build workflows and automations, you’ll need an expert, which makes the ease of learning… not very easy. Your team will also need a significant amount of training to get fully onboarded. Once everything is configured, it becomes easy to use, but getting to that point requires a substantial amount of effort, expertise, and ongoing management.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Asana was one of the first project management tools to make complex workflows feel simple and user-friendly, breaking away from the clunky, enterprise systems that dominated the space. They set the early standard for clean UX, intuitive task management, and thoughtful collaboration features.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"While others chased “all-in-one” features, Asana stayed focused on doing one thing exceptionally well: project management. And even as the industry shifted, they kept pace, especially with their AI implementation. Their AI features feel purposeful rather than gimmicky.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Out of all the long-standing project management tools, Asana has done the best job of integrating AI in a way that feels seamless rather than thrown on for the sake of it. Their AI chat feature is useful, you can move projects and deadlines around just by chatting. They also offer AI teammates through their AI Studio. AI Studio is included on all paid plans with a set credit limit, and you’ll need to purchase additional credits if you exceed that usage, so it’s something to keep in mind.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"What’s particularly interesting about the AI teammates is that you can assign them tasks and they’ll actually complete them, using the context from your Asana account to help you get real work done.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"ClickUp is powerful but overloaded. It can be slow, overwhelming, and easy to get lost in. Users complain that there's a lot of clicking around to just get to the screen that you want and that can cause fatigue. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"ClickUp wasn’t first to market in project management, and instead of doubling down on building the best project manager possible, they went all-in on becoming an all-in-one tool. The result is a massive tool with a ton of features. The issue is that many of those features feel half-baked, more like boxes checked for the marketing page than fully developed functionality. So while they're certainly innovative in aiming to build a comprehensive all-in-one platform, the execution falls short in some areas.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"While ClickUp has implemented AI as a part of their tool, the sentiment seems to be mixed. Some users report that they're glad that ClickUp has AI, but it doesn't seem to be as helpful as they hoped. Folks we spoke to that use ClickUp as their main project management tool have said that they haven't adopted ClickUp's AI which means it's more of a secondary feature rather than a no-brainer to help get work done.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Because ClickUp is so customizable and requires a proper implementation, it also requires significant team training to guarantee team adoption as much as possible. Without proper training and a proper implementation, team adoption will likely be poor. It's not a tool that the team will just naturally jump in to and know how to use. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"ClickUp is incredibly complex to configure as it's highly customizable. Typically, we recommend hiring an agency if you plan on using ClickUp so they can help you configure it. This means having the budget to do so and a dedicated person on your team that can run with the project. It has a steep learning curve.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Jira is more of a project management tool for managers to get reporting to the C-suite. Individual contributors often report Jira reducing their day-to-day productivity because of how clunky the tool is to use, and they need to dedicate so much time fighting to update it for management. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Jira is stagnant in terms of innovation. They are a behemoth old company, built on old tech. A modern company, especially a small business, should not consider using them. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Atlassian, who owns Jira, has launched Atlassian Intelligence, which is their way of implementing AI across a number of tools. It's a bit of a clunky implementation, and seems to only have table stakes AI features (summarizing, search, writing). ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Jira has an incredibly steep learning curve. It even has a certification program just to learn how to set it up. Jira is highly complex, built for enterprises that typically have a dedicated person or team to implement and maintain it. Do not expect it to be easy to learn or use. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Most teams that we speak to hate using Jira. Jira is typically used in enterprises, and upper management forces Jira upon the team. We've spoken to countless people who have used it and absolutely hated it, even considering leaving their role because of how much they hate using it.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Sunsama allows you to pull tasks from popular project management tools like Asana, ClickUp, Monday, Trello, Gmail, and Slack directly into your daily planner, which streamlines bringing all your tasks and calendar events together for daily planning. However, it does not offer an API, so you are limited to the native integrations they provide and cannot connect Sunsama to tools outside this list, which means teams needing custom or broader integrations may find it restrictive.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Sunsama reduces mental clutter by guiding you through a mindful daily planning process, asking each morning what you want to accomplish and showing your tasks alongside your calendar so you can clearly see and organize your priorities. Its minimal, calming design and features like workload limits and daily shutdown routines make it easy to focus on what matters, helping you simplify your plans and next actions without feeling overwhelmed.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Sunsama makes planning your day a simple and mindful process by prompting you each morning to decide what you want to accomplish, which helps you focus on intentional scheduling rather than just reacting to tasks. Its calming, minimal design and easy to use interface mean that even those who prefer pen and paper find it quick to learn and navigate, so you can move your daily planning into the digital space without feeling overwhelmed or lost.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Sunsama relies entirely on manual planning, so when your plans change, you have to adjust your schedule yourself rather than having tasks shift automatically. The app's creators have made it clear they do not plan to add any auto scheduling features, which means you will not get real time adaptive rescheduling and must handle all changes on your own.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Sunsama does not have any AI auto scheduling features and explicitly states that it will not build automatic scheduling into the app. As a result, you need to manually plan and time block your tasks each day, so anyone looking for the app to automatically build their schedule based on priority and availability will not find that capability here.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Wrike was one of the first project management tools on the market that was in the cloud and allowed for team collaboration. That was in 2006. Since then, they have developed strong enterprise features, high-grade security, deep analytics, and reporting, making them an all-around great tool for enterprise businesses. That said, there is no true innovation for small businesses from Wrike. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Enterprise teams or teams with very complex processes say that Wrike improves their productivity in the day-to-day and it helps a lot with team collaboration, especially for remote teams. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Wrike’s AI implementation is pretty much what you’d expect. There’s a built-in chat, which most project management tools offer, and it can generate summaries or help you write content. It’s basic and table stakes at this point.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"One thing that is unique is that Wrike lets you connect your preferred AI app (Claude, ChatGPT, etc.), but it requires using an MCP server, which is really meant for enterprise teams with an IT department to set it up.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Team adoption is only possible with a lot of training and hand-holding. I wouldn't expect a small team to easily adopt this, but if you are an enterprise that has spent hundreds of thousands of dollars on implementation, the success rate is likely higher. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Wrike has a steep learning curve because it’s built for enterprises. Their customer list T-Mobile, Nickelodeon, Walmart, Nvidia makes it clear who they serve. These large organizations have the resources to implement Wrike and value its deep reporting and ability to customize. For small businesses, it's more than you need.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"You get a glimpse of what an AI-native, purpose-driven browser can feel like with Comet, especially if you're the kind of power user who wants an assistant that clicks around the web for you and pulls in email or calendar context. The interface is built to showcase these agentic features in a way that feels more user-friendly than most early AI browsers.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"But the experience is rough around the edges. You run into moments where the assistant can't actually take action on the page you're on, even though the demos suggest it should. The workflow sometimes breaks down when you expect the assistant to handle something and it just can't, so you're still jumping in to finish the job yourself.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"If you're an early adopter who likes trying new things and doesn't mind dealing with unfinished features, you'll appreciate what's here. For anyone expecting a polished, efficient user experience that just works, you're going to feel the inconsistency and friction.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"folk feels immediately familiar if your team is used to spreadsheets or tools like Notion, so small teams can get set up without training or confusion, everyone just gets it and starts adding contacts right away.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"folk feels right at home if you're used to running your CRM out of Google Sheets, Airtable, or Notion. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Impact technically gets the job done when it comes to payments and tax compliance, especially if you’re running a massive, enterprise B2C e-commerce program. All of this infrastructure is built for enterprise-level complexity, not SaaS. For most SaaS companies and smaller teams, the payments setup is overbuilt, unnecessarily expensive, and comes with frustrating onboarding and integration work. So while Impact is capable here, it is inefficient, heavy, and completely misaligned with what the majority of SaaS companies actually need.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Impact’s tracking is fundamentally limited for modern SaaS use cases. This frequently results in clicks being logged without conversions, conversions happening without attribution, and partners losing credit entirely. These issues lead to unreliable data, partner disputes, and broken trust across the program.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"PartnerStack centralizes commission payouts, collects W-8/W-9 forms, and issues 1099-NECs when you use PartnerStack-managed payments. In practice, if you are using PartnerStack, it makes the most sense to use PartnerStack for payments to keep payouts and tax compliance fully consolidated. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"tag":"h4","type":"heading","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Multiple Checking & Savings Accounts","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null},{"type":"paragraph","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo gives you a single business checking account per entity, but you can create up to 20 \"Reserves,\" which are internal budgeting buckets for things like taxes, payroll, profit, and expenses. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"But heads up, Reserves are not separate accounts or sub‑accounts, they have no unique account/routing numbers, all funds remain part of one Total Balance.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"tag":"h4","type":"heading","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Auto-Transfer Rules","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null},{"type":"paragraph","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo Reserves let you automatically allocate a percentage of each incoming deposit into specific Reserves, which is good for simple Profit‑First‑style budgeting (e.g., 25% of all future deposits to Taxes). However, those rules only move money inside the one checking account into non‑account \"envelopes\". You can't set target balances like with Mercury and Relay (e.g. when my envelope reaches X amount, transfer money).","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"tag":"h4","type":"heading","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Multiple Business Accounts","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null},{"type":"paragraph","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo lets you open a separate business checking account for each business as long as each has its own EIN, but you need a separate login for each entity, so there’s no single dashboard where you can quickly toggle between multiple businesses under one username.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo is business‑only, there is no Novo personal banking product.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"tag":"h4","type":"heading","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"User roles, Permissions, and Guardrails ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null},{"type":"paragraph","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"With Novo, when you add another user to your account, you're granting them ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1},{"mode":"normal","text":"full access ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":1,"version":1},{"mode":"normal","text":"and account‑owner‑level privileges. This means they can transfer and withdraw funds since Novo doesn't offer partial or read‑only access.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"This is much less safe and protected than other banking solutions out there, which is why if you have a team we don't recommend using Novo.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"tag":"h4","type":"heading","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Approvals","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null},{"type":"paragraph","format":"start","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo does not have rule‑based approvals for payments, again making it subpar for any business with a growing team.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"tag":"h4","type":"heading","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Paying Vendors","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"While you can add payees (vendors, contractors) to Novo, there is no self-service vendor onboarding like the other banks have (e.g. no secure link where vendors can enter in their payment details and no automatic collection of W-9s).","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Unlike with other banks, Novo won't automatically scrape your invoices and enter them into the system. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo's target audience seems to be more freelancers who don't need to often make payments to others, so if you're a growing business, skip Novo. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"tag":"h4","type":"heading","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Checks","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo will write and mail checks on your behalf, however if you need to attach a PDF or form, you're out of luck and will need to figure out another way outside of Novo. ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"tag":"h4","type":"heading","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Getting Paid","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo offers basic invoicing capabilities, although the user experience is not as smooth as with other banks.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

{"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo has no interest-bearing savings account and no treasury product. {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo it works like a normal business checking account: once you've given someone your routing and account numbers, they can initiate ACH pulls into or out of your account, and you’re relying on your own monitoring plus Novo's dispute process if something sketchy shows up.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"With that, Novo covers the basics for fund safety (coverage up to $250K), but you do not get extra protection beyond standard FDIC insurance.
